findings.description: The HTML includes fabricated boilerplate and styling metadata not present in the source PDF, including a page title and imported theme/CSS branding.
findings.evidence: HTML contains '<title>Converted Document</title>' and CSS comments/text 'Digital Commons NC — Standalone Theme CSS', '@import url(...)', and style variable names such as '--nc-font', '--nc-navy'. These do not appear anywhere in the PDF pages, which only show the fact sheet text.

findings.description: Heading hierarchy does not reflect the document structure. The document title block from the PDF was not represented as a single top-level heading, and section headings were converted to h2 without a corresponding h1.
findings.evidence:
PDF title block: 'FACT SHEET / DRAFT PERMIT RENEWAL / Safety-Kleen Archdale / 6182 Old Mendenhall Road / Archdale, North Carolina / EPA ID Number: NCD 077 840 148'. HTML places this in repeated '

...

' blocks, while only 'Background' and 'Public Comment & Public Hearing' are marked as '

'.

findings.description: Header content is duplicated in the HTML by repeating the full title/address block for both pages, rather than consolidating or preserving it once as document metadata/content.
findings.evidence: HTML repeats the same page-header text twice: 'FACT SHEET ... EPA ID Number: NCD 077 840 148' before each page section. In the PDF this appears as page header formatting on each page, not as duplicated body content.
